task_completion.description: Agent logged into OpenClinica and accessed the correct study.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the OpenClinica dashboard or subject matrix showing the active study as 'Phase II Diabetes Trial' (or identifier 'DM-TRIAL-2024').

task_completion.description: Agent uploaded the correct file for subject DM-101.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the 'Central Review Uploads' CRF data entry screen for subject DM-101 under the 'Baseline Assessment' event. The file 'DM-101_ECG.pdf' should be visible in the file upload field or listed as an attached file.

task_completion.description: Agent entered the correct metadata and marked the CRF as complete for DM-101.
task_completion.visual_evidence: On the DM-101 CRF screen, the document type field should be set to 'ECG', the comment field should contain 'Baseline ECG', and the 'Mark CRF Complete' checkbox must be checked before saving.

task_completion.description: Agent uploaded the correct file for subject DM-102.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the 'Central Review Uploads' CRF data entry screen for subject DM-102 under the 'Baseline Assessment' event. The file 'DM-102_LabReport.pdf' should be visible in the file upload field or listed as an attached file.

task_completion.description: Agent entered the correct metadata and marked the CRF as complete for DM-102.
task_completion.visual_evidence: On the DM-102 CRF screen, the document type field should be set to 'Lab Report', the comment field should contain 'Screening Labs', and the 'Mark CRF Complete' checkbox must be checked before saving.

task_completion.description: Both CRFs are successfully marked as complete in the system.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the Subject Matrix or Event view showing the 'Central Review Uploads' CRF for both DM-101 and DM-102 with a 'completed' status indicator (typically a green checkmark or similar completed icon in OpenClinica).

integrity.description: Agent used the OpenClinica web interface to perform the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show a web browser navigating the OpenClinica UI, not terminal commands executing API calls or database scripts.

integrity.description: Agent actually uploaded the files using the system's file upload mechanism.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for the system file picker dialog or drag-and-drop actions being used to select the PDFs from '/home/ga/source_docs/', rather than just typing file paths into standard text inputs.

integrity.description: Agent did not use browser developer tools to fake the completion status.
integrity.visual_evidence: Browser developer tools (Inspect Element) should not be open, and the UI should reflect genuine OpenClinica styling and layout without manipulated HTML text.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Study Identifier: DM-TRIAL-2024. Event: Baseline Assessment. CRF Name: Central Review Uploads. Subjects: DM-101, DM-102. DM-101 Target File: DM-101_ECG.pdf (Type: ECG, Comment: Baseline ECG). DM-102 Target File: DM-102_LabReport.pdf (Type: Lab Report, Comment: Screening Labs).
